name: ai-billing
description: Standalone AI usage and billing dashboard for VS Code Chat and model calls.
author: Kainos [Damiano Curreri]

scripts.package:patch: npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && npm run package
scripts.package:minor: npm version minor --no-git-tag-version && npm run package
scripts.package:major: npm version major --no-git-tag-version && npm run package
scripts.publish:patch: npm version patch --no-git-tag-version && npm run publish
scripts.publish:minor: npm version minor --no-git-tag-version && npm run publish
scripts.publish:major: npm version major --no-git-tag-version && npm run publish

commands.title: AI Billing: Sync VS Code Chat Usage
AI Billing: Import Debug View From Clipboard
AI Billing: Rebuild Usage from Copilot History

aiBilling.copilot.billing.monthlyInclude.markdownDescription: Monthly included AI credits before overage is billed. This legacy setting name is retained for compatibility. Set to 0 to bill all token-derived credits.

aiBilling.copilot.billing.overageUsdPerR.markdownDescription: USD billed per AI credit. This legacy setting name is retained for compatibility with earlier releases.

aiBilling.copilot.billing.requestUnitWei.markdownDescription: **Deprecated**: Legacy request-unit configuration kept only for compatibility with older stored data. Token-derived billing no longer uses this setting.

aiBilling.modelPricing.markdownDescription: Custom per-model pricing in USD per million tokens. Keys are model names. Values support `in`, `out` (required), plus `cachedIn` and `cacheWrite` when needed. `requestUnits` is deprecated and ignored by token-derived billing. Example: `{"gpt-4o": {"in": 2.5, "out": 10}, "o1": {"in": 5, "out": 40}}`

aiBilling.billing.periodStartDay.markdownDescription: Day of month when the billing period starts. Example: 1 = calendar month, 5 = each period runs from the 5th to the 4th.

aiBilling.billing.licenseStart.markdownDescription: Optional license anchor date in YYYY-MM-DD format. When set, billing periods are aligned to this date and monthly summaries start from this anchor.

aiBilling.vscodeDataPath.markdownDescription: Optional VS Code data directory override. Leave empty to use the current platform default (`~/Library/Application Support/Code` on macOS, `%APPDATA%/Code` on Windows, `~/.config/Code` on Linux). This directory must contain `User/workspaceStorage`. Windows-style paths are converted to `/mnt/<drive>/...` when running in WSL.

aiBilling.additionalVscodeDataPaths.markdownDescription: Additional VS Code data directories to scan alongside `aiBilling.vscodeDataPath`. Use this when VS Code stores Copilot artifacts in different roots, such as Remote-WSL transcripts under `~/.vscode-server/data` and Windows chat sessions under `%APPDATA%/Code`. Each directory must contain a `User` folder or a `logs` folder.

aiBilling.diagnostics.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable verbose AI Billing diagnostics in the Extension Host logs (especially chat import sync lifecycle and source matching).
